“It was now winter, and Jesus was in Jerusalem at the time of Hanukkah, the Festival of Dedication. He was in the Temple, walking through the section known as Solomon’s Colonnade. The people surrounded him and asked, “How long are you going to keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.” Jesus replied, “I have already told you, and you don’t believe me. The proof is the work I do in my Father’s name. But you don’t believe me because you are not my sheep. My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me. I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one can snatch them away from me, for my Father has given them to me, and he is more powerful than anyone else. No one can snatch them from the Father’s hand. The Father and I are one.””

[25/06/2025, 4:44:18 PM] Michael Mitry: I. Quick Prep (before cooking)
•
Thinly slice one pound of top sirloin steak. For best results, use a high-quality steak. Boneless ribeye or beef tenderloin will also work well.
•
Thickly slice half a pound of white or brown mushrooms.
•
Finely chop half of a medium onion. The onion will shrink down and disappear into the sauce.
•
Finely mince one large garlic clove.
II. Cooking the Beef Stroganoff
1.
Sear the Beef:
◦
Place a large deep pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
◦
Add two tablespoons of oil. Once the oil is hot, add the beef strips in a single layer.
◦
Sear the beef without moving it around for about one minute per side or just until cooked through and no longer red.
◦
Remove the seared beef right away to a separate bowl and cover to keep warm.
◦
Sear the remaining beef in batches to prevent the pan from cooling down too quickly, which can make the beef juice out and become chewy.
◦
Transfer all cooked beef to the bowl and cover to keep warm.
2.
Sauté Onions and Mushrooms:
◦
Melt two tablespoons of butter in the pan.
◦
Add the chopped onion and sliced mushrooms.
◦
Sauté, stirring occasionally, for six to seven minutes or until all of the liquid has evaporated and the onions and mushrooms are soft and lightly browned.
3.
Add Garlic and Flour:
◦
Add your minced garlic clove and sauté for another minute or until fragrant.
◦
Sprinkle one tablespoon of flour over the top and sauté for another minute, stirring constantly.
4.
Build the Sauce Base:
◦
Pour in one cup of beef broth and stir, scraping the bottom of the pan.
◦
Next, add three-fourths cup of heavy whipping cream and bring everything to a simmer.
5.
Temper and Add Sour Cream:
◦
Stir a few tablespoons of the simmering sauce into your sour cream to temper it, which helps prevent it from curdling.
◦
Then, add the tempered sour cream to the pan while whisking constantly.
6.
Season the Sauce:
◦
Stir in one tablespoon of Worcestershire sauce.
◦
Add half a teaspoon of Dijon mustard.
◦
Season with half a teaspoon of salt and a quarter teaspoon of black pepper, or season to taste.
◦
Bring the sauce back to a simmer; it should be creamy.
7.
Combine Beef with Sauce:
◦
Add the cooked beef back to the pan along with any accumulated juices.
◦
Bring it back just to a simmer or until the beef is heated through.
III. Serving
•
While the stroganoff is cooking, boil egg noodles according to package instructions.
•
Once the egg noodles are cooked, drain them well and set them aside until you are ready to serve.
•
Serve the beef stroganoff with the egg noodles. It also pairs well with mashed potatoes or rice because of its "amazing gravy".
•
Garnish with a bunch of chopped fresh chives for a "great pop of flavor and color".
The stroganoff is described as "so saucy" and will thicken up a little more when it cools down. It's an "easy and excellent 30-minute dinner".

[28/06/2025, 1:09:28 AM] Michael Mitry: For a baby with anemia, the most direct form of volume expansion is typically the transfusion of packed red blood cells (RBCs), as anemia is defined by a low hemoglobin or hematocrit (indicating a reduced number of red blood cells).
Here's a breakdown of volume expansion and its relevance to neonatal anemia:
•
Packed Red Blood Cell Transfusion for Anemia
◦
Indications: Packed RBCs are transfused for symptomatic anemia or very low hemoglobin/hematocrit levels.
▪
Asymptomatic infants with a hematocrit value below 18% (hemoglobin < 6g/dl).
▪
Symptomatic infants on mechanical ventilation with a hematocrit value between 25-30%.
▪
Infants with significant respiratory disease or congenital heart disease.
▪
Infants with ABO incompatibility.
◦
This directly replaces the deficient red blood cells and increases the oxygen-carrying capacity of the blood, while also contributing to overall blood volume.
•
Volume Expanders for Associated Conditions (e.g., Hypovolemia or Shock)
◦
While not directly treating the anemia (lack of RBCs) itself, other volume expanders are crucial if the anemia is due to acute blood loss leading to hypovolemia or shock.
◦
Normal Saline: This is a common volume expander used for hypovolemia or in babies not responding to resuscitation.
▪
It helps to increase vascular volume and can improve metabolic acidosis by enhancing tissue perfusion.
▪
The recommended dose is 10 mL/kg of normal saline administered intravenously over 5-10 minutes, typically via the umbilical vein.
◦
Fresh Frozen Plasma (FFP): This can be given intravenously at a dose of 10 mL/kg for active bleeding (a cause of anemia) and can be repeated as needed. FFP provides clotting factors and expands volume.
◦
Fresh Whole Blood: For bleeding, fresh whole blood can be administered at 10 mL/kg, with additional amounts as needed. This would provide both red blood cells and plasma components, thus expanding volume.
•
Context of other conditions:
◦
In cases of hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy (HIE), treatment for hypotension may involve volume and inotropic support. If blood loss contributes to this hypotension, then the above volume expanders would be relevant.
◦
In the management of polycythemia (a condition of excessively high red blood cell count, opposite to anemia), saline is used as a replacement fluid during partial exchange transfusion to reduce the hematocrit. This demonstrates saline's role in blood volume adjustment, but in a different clinical scenario.
Therefore, for anemia, the most appropriate "volume expansion" typically refers to the transfusion of packed red blood cells. If the anemia is complicated by acute blood loss and hypovolemia, then crystalloids like normal saline, or blood products like FFP or whole blood, may also be used to restore circulatory volume

[28/07/2025, 12:30:10 PM] My Princess 👸❤️😘: Absolutely. The story of **Paul** (formerly **Saul**) is one of the most powerful transformations in the Bible — a man who was violently opposed to Jesus, personally *confronted and invited* by Him, and then became one of His most devoted followers.

This is not just a story of conversion — it’s a **deep, personal relationship** between a broken man and a patient Savior.

---

## ✨ **Paul: The Enemy Who Became the Friend of God**

---

### 🔥 **CHAPTER 1: Saul – The Religious Zealot**

Saul was a brilliant, passionate, highly educated Pharisee from Tarsus. He believed with all his heart that the Jesus movement was dangerous, heretical, and dishonoring to God.

> “Breathing out murderous threats…” *(Acts 9:1)*
> He led the persecution of early Christians, dragging them from homes, imprisoning them, and even standing by when Stephen was stoned to death.

But Saul wasn’t just angry — he was *convinced* he was right. He thought he was serving God by silencing Jesus’ followers.

**He was zealous — but blind.**

---

### 🌟 **CHAPTER 2: The Damascus Encounter — A Divine Interruption**

**Acts 9:1–19**

While on his way to **Damascus** to arrest more Christians, Saul’s journey was stopped by **light from heaven** — so bright, it knocked him to the ground.

And then he heard a voice — not angry, but **personal**:

> **“Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?”**

Saul was stunned:

> “Who are you, Lord?”

> **“I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.”**

That moment broke him.

He was blinded for three days — helpless, humbled, undone.

God then sent a Christian man named **Ananias** (who was terrified!) to heal Saul and deliver a message:

> **“Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus… has sent me so you may see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”**
> *(Acts 9:17)*

And immediately, something like scales fell from his eyes. Saul could see again — but nothing would ever look the same.

---

### 💔 **CHAPTER 3: Paul’s Surrender and Friendship with Jesus**

Saul’s name eventually became **Paul**, and everything changed.
He was no longer motivated by pride or tradition — but by **love** and a **deep, personal relationship** with Christ.

> **“I consider everything a loss because of the surpassing worth of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord…”**
> *(Philippians 3:8)*

He didn’t just know *about* Jesus. He knew Him — and called Him **“my Lord.”**

---

### ✝️ **CHAPTER 4: A Life Poured Out for Love**

Paul’s new life wasn’t easy.

* He was beaten, imprisoned, shipwrecked, betrayed, and hated.
* He was misunderstood by many — even Christians.
* But he *never stopped loving Jesus*.

> **“I no longer live, but Christ lives in me.”**
> *(Galatians 2:20)*

He planted churches, wrote letters (which became much of the New Testament), and spent his life helping others know the One who changed him.

Even near death, his heart burned with intimacy:

> **“I want to know Christ — yes, to know the power of His resurrection and participation in His sufferings…”**
> *(Philippians 3:10)*

---

## ❤️ **Why Paul’s Story Is So Personal**

* Jesus **called him by name** — not to punish, but to invite.
* Paul’s whole life was redefined by **a relationship**, not a religion.
* He went from pride to surrender, violence to love, fear to faith.

God didn’t just *forgive* Paul — He **trusted him** with the gospel.
And Paul responded not with duty, but **devotion**.

---

## 🕊️ **What This Means for Us**

* No one is too far gone.
* God doesn’t just want to fix you — He wants to **know you**.
* You don’t need to prove yourself — just respond to His voice.

> **“Saul, Saul…”**
> He still calls us — by name — into **friendship, purpose, and love**.

---

Would you like me to:

* Turn this into a **spoken word or monologue** as if Paul is telling his story?
* Create a **reflection journal** to help you connect personally with Paul’s journey?
* Make a comparison between Paul and another biblical figure who was invited into relationship?

Let me know what would help you go deeper!

وأجي أديلك عمري اللي جاي.

ربي شايفني طول اليوم.
وقت ما أنامي وبعد ما أقوم.
ده إنت شايلني جوة عينيك.
ده إنت ناقشني على كفيك.
ربي شايفني طول اليوم.
وقت ما أنامي.
وبعد ما أوم.
دا أنت شايلني جوة عينيك.
دا أنت ناقشني على كفيك.
هيه هيه هيه هيه هيه.
عمرك حسيت إنك لوحدك محدش حاسس بيك وباللي في قلبك حتى لو مع أقرب الناس ليك حتى لو كانت الحاجات دي إنت شايفها هايفة أو مش هتهم ربنا.

Ana 3ayez a2olak haga… yesterday was honestly one of the most special days of my life 🥹✨. It wasn’t just an outing 🫣, it wasn’t just laughs, it wasn’t just seeing your smile (ely btw ana lazem akteb fi Guinness records enno it’s the cutest smile alive 😏😂). Yesterday felt like a new chapter, the kind of chapter you highlight in a book 3ashan mafish shak enno it’s one that will never end, it’s just the beginning of our whole story 🫠

Enty te3rafy kanet special leh? 3ashan it was our 300-day mark ❤️. 300 days ya Douri!!! 300 days of ana we enty, of me waiting for your messages, of your little jokes that make me laugh like a crazy person 🤪, of your care even when you’re dead tired. Ana bshof how you’re growing, fighting, pushing through kol haga around you, smart, beautiful, strong, future doctor astronaut architect you 😚. 300 days of me realizing every single yom enno enty not just important to me, enti literally my peace, my safe place, my best story, my forever 😍

We add to all of that ana met your parents 🥹🤍. Let me tell you, I was nervous 😅 ana el pilot elly biyet3alem ezay y-handle crosswinds fi 40kt gusts, bas standing in front of your parents felt easier than I thought 😂. Lama shoftek smiling, suddenly I felt safe. Not because it was easy, bas 3ashan you were there. With you, everything feels easy ❤️

Sandra, enti msh sha5s ba7eb akalemoh we khalas, enti my morning thought, my cockpit daydream, my coffee moment, my before sleep prayer 🙏. You’re the face that comes fi bali when I dream big ✈️, the one I imagine clapping for me after an exam 📚🔥, the one I see next to me on boring long-hauls when I’m overthinking 🤭

Ba7eb feeki your fiery ambition 🔥, bas at the same time your softness 🕊️. Ba7eb how your laugh sounds like background music, like my vitamin Dodo that I constantly need and never get bored of ❤️. Even when you’re annoyed, you still care 🥺. Even when you’re tired, you still check 3alaya. Ba7eb the way you trust me with your feelings, and even when words are hard, you let me just be there 😘. Im so lucky you’re in my life, batates 🥔❤️

Tomorrow ana raye7 Cyprus again 🧳✈️, bas 3ayezek te3rafy enno yesterday is really special fi alby, like a treasure. Nothing will change that. Enty ma3aya in every study hour, ma3aya fi cockpit, ma3aya fi kol ra7a w kol ta3ab. W honestly, every time I look outside the aircraft window w ashoof the clouds ☁️, I’ll think of you. Because enti are my sky, and even the clouds look more beautiful when you’re there 😉

W خلينا n3raf el truth, ana we enty after 300 days? That’s just the trailer 😏🔥. The real movie lesa btebda2. Picture this: exams finished ✅, you smashing your medical career 👩‍⚕️💪, me flying el Airbus or Boeing ✈️, w both of us celebrating in some cute coffee shop bel 7agat elly enti bet7ebeeha 🧋. Bas until then, han3mel party for every small win 🎉. Always celebrating you and me together 😍 because we’re one team, and that team has a name now… Marldouri ❤️ Marble + Douri = forever 😘

Ana msh 3ayezak tensi, enti my constant ❤️, my sunshine ☀️, my comfort 🕊️, my Douri 🐣, my favorite notification on my phone 📲, my guilty smile in class lama my phone suddenly lights up 🤭, my prayer answered 🫠. Tu es mon trésor, ma paix, mon sourire préféré 😘

So thank you for yesterday, thank you for the last 300 days, thank you for being you. Ana hafdal henaaaa, always your Marble, your Miko, your crazy pilot fanboy reporting for duty 🤪✈️🐶

Good morning ya 7abibti 😘
I hope your day shines zay the way you shine fi alby, every single yom ❤️‍🔥

Always yours,
Miko ✈️❤️😘

[24/08/2025, 6:13:55 PM] Michael Mitry: 🌱 Relationship Growth
	•	Looking back at our 300 days, what has been your favorite moment with me?
	•	What’s something I do that makes you feel most loved?
	•	Is there something new you’d like us to try together (a tradition, hobby, trip)?
	•	What do you think we’ve both learned about each other since day 1?

⸻

💌 Future Together
	•	When you imagine us after your 3 years of med school and my pilot training, what does our life look like?
	•	What’s a dream date or trip you’d want us to take once we’re both done studying?
	•	If we had a place that’s just “ours,” what would you want it to look like?
	•	Do you see yourself more excited about building a home in Egypt, Europe, or somewhere else?

⸻

💭 Deeper Feelings
	•	When was the moment you first realized you loved me?
	•	What’s something you’ve wanted to tell me but maybe felt shy to say?
	•	What do you think makes our bond different from other couples?
	•	If you had to describe me in three words to someone new, what would they be?

⸻

🎯 Support & Daily Life
	•	What’s the hardest part of med school for you right now, and how can I support you better?
	•	Do you like how we balance calls/texts, or do you wish something was different?
	•	When you’re stressed, what’s the best thing I can say or do for you?
	•	Is there a small daily ritual you’d like us to start (like a good morning note, or end-of-day call)?

[18/09/2025, 4:22:09 PM] Michael Mitry: Main Findings:
	•	Supraspinatus and Subscapularis Tendinopathy
→ This means there is irritation/degeneration (tendinopathy) of two of the rotator cuff tendons (supraspinatus and subscapularis).
→ Importantly: no full-thickness tear or rupture was seen, which is good news.
	•	Mild Joint Effusion
→ A small amount of extra fluid inside the shoulder joint (sign of inflammation/irritation).
	•	Minimal Bursal Distention (subacromial and subcoracoid bursae)
→ Slight fluid in the bursae (fluid-filled sacs that cushion the tendons), again due to irritation/inflammation.
	•	Other Normal Structures
→ No tear in the labrum.
→ Biceps tendon looks normal.
→ No dislocation or major structural damage.

What This Means:
	•	This is not a severe injury, but rather an overuse or strain injury of the shoulder tendons.
	•	It explains shoulder pain, weakness, or discomfort, especially with overhead movements.
	•	It’s treatable with rest, physiotherapy, anti-inflammatory measures, and activity modification.
	•	Surgery is not indicated since there’s no tear.

👉 In short:
It’s shoulder tendinopathy with mild inflammation (but no tendon tear).

أتمنى تكونوا كويسين😊 

اولاً برحب بيكم فى السنة الجديدة فى النشاط ٢٠٢٥-٢٠٢٦🙏🏽 

ثانياً هطلب منك تبعتولى إذا فى مسؤولين جدد هيستلموا المدرسة السنة دى علشان ندخلهم الجروب🙏🏽 

ثالثاً متحمسين جداً إننا نتقابل فى أقرب وقت، نرجع نتعرف على بعض و على الأعضاء الجديدة فى الفريق السنة دى قبل إنطلاقة السنة مع الرواد 
و علشان كدة عايزين نتقابل فى أول إجتماع لينا يوم الجمعة ٢٦ سبتمبر من  الساعة ٥:٣٠ ل ٩:٣٠ فى الجيزويت 

كمان فى الإجتماع ده هنكون بنتعرف على شعار السنة و هنشارك معاكم تفاصيل إنطلاقة الرواد و أجندة السنة.

و لحد مانتقابل.. حابيين نبدأ نبلغ الرواد بانطلاقة السنة اللى هتكون يوم ١٦ و ١٧ اكتوبر فى شكل معسكرات لكل فرع و هنتجمع يوم الجمعة كلنا فى الآخر نختم بقداس🙏🏽 ( هنبعت الإستمارة و التفاصيل قريب ) 

متحمسين و فى إنتظار كل شخص فى الفريق علشان نبدأ السنة مع بعض 🙏🏽
